checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: SalesRevenueNet, FY2010 annual (year-end AR rose ~2.2% while FY revenue fell ~10.4%; intra-year AR is highly seasonal, e.g. 3233M at 2010-05-31, limiting confidence this reflects misstatement rather than timing)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.location: Goodwill FY2010 (intangibles declined ~7.9%, goodwill roughly flat -0.4%, versus revenue decline of 10.4%; OtherAssetsNoncurrent grew modestly 496M->524M from a small base) - no soft-asset outpacing observed

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: FY2010 annual NI vs OCF - OCF meets or exceeds NI in every available fiscal year, inconsistent with earnings inflation

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.location: InventoryNet FY2009 vs FY2010 declined only 6.6% vs 10.4% revenue decline; gross margin compressed sharply from ~57.7% to ~48.4%, indicating margin/turnover pressure likely tied to real pricing dynamics rather than smoothing

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: Quarterly NI swings around FY2010/2011 - large swings including losses track seasonal revenue pattern, indicating no artificial smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: AR Q2 FY2011 - allowance fell ~49% (218M->111M) from FY2008 to Feb-2011 even as gross AR grew (2377M vs 2093M a year earlier), coverage ratio dropping from ~14% to under 5%, possibly indicating reserve release supporting earnings

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.location: filing_chronology - no NT 10-Q/10-K or 10-K/A restatements observed; periodic reports filed on regular schedule; a few 8-K/A amendments are not unusual for a company of this size

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.location: Earliest annual revenue data point (FY2008) - dataset provides only ~3 full fiscal years of income statement data (FY2008-FY2010) plus partial FY2011 quarters, missing tax, segment, and reserve footnote detail needed for deeper forensic review

top_signals: Declining allowance for doubtful accounts (218M FY2008 -> 143M FY2010 -> 111M at 2011-02-28) even as gross accounts receivable grew into 2011 (2377M at 2011-02-28 vs 2093M a year earlier), suggesting possible reserve release supporting reported results
Year-end accounts receivable grew slightly (1556M FY2009 -> 1590M FY2010) while annual revenue fell materially (11724M -> 10502M), an unusual divergence though partly explainable by seasonality
Sharp gross margin compression (57.7% FY2009 -> 48.4% FY2010) alongside inventory declining less than revenue, indicating real margin/cost pressure rather than a smoothing artifact

Offsetting positive signals: operating cash flow met or exceeded net income in every available fiscal year (e.g., OCF=2837M vs NI=2024M in FY2008; OCF=1398M vs NI=1109M in FY2010), and quarterly earnings show natural, unsmoothed seasonal volatility consistent with the agricultural business cycle
No NT filings, restatements, or unusual 8-K spikes were observed in the filing chronology through the cutoff date
